---
title: "Data"
---

Data source: Meteoroloji Genel Müdürlüğü, TÜİK, NASA


## 1. General Information About Data
The "Drought Risk Analysis" project focuses on the intersection of environmental stress and economic resilience in Türkiye. By analyzing long-term climate trends alongside agricultural output, we aim to uncover the hidden costs of climate change.

### 1.1 Why We Selected This Data
We selected this dataset for the EMU430 course because it allows us to quantify the environmental shifts and their correlation with agricultural output. Understanding how daily precipitation and temperature affect the monetary value of crops is essential for Turkey's sustainable development.

### 1.2  Our Objectives
To analyze the steady climb of regional temperatures and its impact on water stress.  To differentiate between nominal agricultural growth and real production value after adjusting for inflation.  To investigate the "Correlation Paradox" between rainfall amounts and crop value.  


## 2.Data Preprocessing
### 2.1 Dataset Overview
-Thermal Data (T2M): Average temperature at 2 meters above the earth's surface,

-Hydrological Data (PRECTOTCORR): Corrected daily precipitation amounts ($mm/day$)

-Agricultural Value: The monetary value of crop production (Thousand TL) at provincial and regional levels.
